summary: Iteration 5 — Batch B backend-only verification. 72/72 pytest cases PASSED (100%) including 9 new tests in test_priorities_done.py. (1) DailyEntry priorities_done round-trips perfectly: POST with [True,False,False] returns same array; GET by date returns same; PUT to [True,True,False] persists and GET confirms. Default empty list when omitted. (2) PEPPER checkin priorities_done preservation works: first call on a fresh user initializes priorities_done length-matched to top_priorities with all False values; after user toggles index 0 → True and a second same-day checkin runs (with a longer dump producing potentially more priorities), index 0 remains True — not reset. (3) Tasks (loops) full CRUD verified end-to-end including PUT status='done' and DELETE cleanup with deadline field round-trip. (4) Regression sanity green for GET /api/tasks, GET /api/money-entries, GET /api/body-logs, POST /api/pepper/advise-person (verdict in trust|caution|cut), POST /api/pepper/advise-body (returns vibe_read, care_moves[], permission). Full suite re-run: 72 passed in 49.57s.

minor.issue: No server-side validation that len(priorities_done) == len(top_priorities). A client could send 5 priorities and 2 done flags (or vice versa) and the server happily persists the mismatched arrays. Frontend pepper_checkin re-aligns them on its side, but direct POST/PUT does not. Consider a Pydantic model_validator. Reference: /app/backend/server.py:80-91.

When existing entry has priorities_done shorter than new top_priorities, extras are padded with False (correct), but when prior priorities_done is LONGER than new top_priorities (AI returned fewer items), the tail is silently truncated. Acceptable for MVP but worth noting — users may lose 'done' state if AI slims the list. Reference: /app/backend/server.py:500-511.
ObjectId() casts still raise bson.errors.InvalidId → HTTP 500 instead of 404/400 on malformed ids in PUT/DELETE/GET-by-id endpoints. Not blocking.

action_items: Optional: add Pydantic model_validator to DailyEntryCreate that pads/truncates priorities_done to match len(top_priorities), or rejects with 422 on mismatch.
Optional (Batch B follow-up): if AI ever returns fewer than the prior top_priorities count, decide product behavior — keep prior 'done' flags vs. discard. Currently they're truncated.
Carry-over from iter 4: wrap ObjectId() casts to return 404 on malformed ids; split server.py (1140 lines) into routers (pepper.py, money.py, body.py, daily.py).
critical_code_review_comments: DailyEntryCreate.priorities_done: List[bool] = [] and DailyEntryResponse.priorities_done: List[bool] = [] are correctly typed; mongo round-trip preserves booleans as booleans (not 0/1).

pepper_checkin re-alignment logic at server.py:500-511 correctly preserves prior 'done' values by index and pads with False on growth. Truncation on shrink is implicit (loop is bounded by len(new_priorities)) — flagged above as minor.
Tasks endpoints unchanged from prior iteration; deadline field passes through as Optional[str] without date validation (intentional — matches deadline field design).
server.py now ~1140 lines — still above the 700-line guideline.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_pepper_pdone_<timestamp> user in ai_checkins + daily_entries collections (2 checkins, 1 daily entry). Created daily entries for default_user on dates 2030-01-XX and 2030-02-15. Created and DELETED one task titled TEST_loop_task for default_user. No DELETE endpoint for daily-entries means the 2030-dated entries persist; not user-visible on Today screen (different date).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL is in /app/frontend/.env (EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL). All 72 backend tests pass in ~50s. Batch B priorities_done feature verified end-to-end: POST/GET/PUT round-trip on /api/daily-entries, plus PEPPER checkin init+preserve+pad semantics. New tests live in tests/test_priorities_done.py. Known minor gap: server does not validate priorities_done length against top_priorities length on direct POST/PUT — only pepper_checkin re-aligns them.
rca_of_issue: No bugs found in Batch B. Both priorities_done CRUD and the checkin preservation/padding logic behave per spec.
